Container.Name: Homey-Wan-Kenobi-MCP
Container.Overview: Independent, self-hosted Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for Homey Pro smart home systems. Gives an MCP client (Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cline, Kiro) natural-language control of devices, zones, flows and Advanced Flows, live and historical energy, Insights, Z-Wave and Zigbee mesh diagnostics, and system operations, over 60 tools. Not affiliated with, endorsed by, or supported by Athom or Homey. Set MCP_TRANSPORT=streamable-http (default here) to expose the service at http://[IP]:[PORT:8000]/mcp with health at /healthz. The /mcp endpoint has NO built-in auth; keep it on a trusted LAN or behind an authenticating reverse proxy. Create a Homey local API key at https://my.homey.app/settings/system/api-keys and set HOMEY_ADDRESS and HOMEY_TOKEN.
Container.Description: Self-hosted MCP server for Homey Pro: 60 tools for devices, flows, energy, and Z-Wave/Zigbee diagnostics. Streamable-HTTP at /mcp. Independent project, not affiliated with Athom or Homey.

Config.Name: MCP HTTP Port
Config.Description: Host port for the MCP streamable-http endpoint (served at /mcp, health at /healthz).

Config.Name: HOMEY_ADDRESS
Config.Description: Base URL of your Homey Pro local API, e.g. http://192.168.1.10

Config.Name: HOMEY_TOKEN
Config.Description: Homey local API key, created at https://my.homey.app/settings/system/api-keys. Authenticates this server to Homey.

Config.Name: MCP_TRANSPORT
Config.Description: Leave as streamable-http for the persistent HTTP service. Set to stdio only if launching the container interactively as an MCP client.

Config.Name: MCP_PORT
Config.Description: Container-internal port the HTTP service listens on. Match the container side of the port mapping.
